title: Lilly Foundayo — T2D Filing Deadline Arrives; Obesity Launch Imminent
headline: Eli Lilly is expected to file Foundayo (orforglipron) for type 2 diabetes with the FDA by end of Q2 (June 30), while the obesity-approved version prepares for launch with $50/month Medicare Part D pricing from July 1.
key_facts: Foundayo approved for obesity April 1, 2026 via FDA's Commissioner's National Priority Voucher (CNPV); NDA received January 20, 2026 (NDA 220934); indicated for adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related medical problems; starting dose 0.8 mg once daily, escalated to 2.5 mg after >=30 days, then 5.5 mg, may increase to 9 mg, 14.5 mg or 17.2 mg; $149/month for lowest dose; eligible Medicare Part D individuals may get it for $50/month beginning July 1, 2026; T2D filing due end of Q2 2026; three pivotal Phase 3 ACHIEVE trials at ADA showed superior A1C control and weight loss vs oral semaglutide (June 8, 2026); LLY ~$1,204.76 as of July 1 close, down from ~$1,229.93 on June 29 (~2.0-2.8% decline); Innovent Biologics and Lilly entered a Verzen distribution and promotion agreement (June 30); only GLP-1 pill with no time, food or water restrictions

title: Vistagen Crashes Over 70% on PALISADE-4 Phase 3 Failure
headline: Vistagen's stock plunged more than 70% to close at $0.22 after PALISADE-4 failed its primary endpoint in social anxiety disorder, mirroring the earlier PALISADE-3 miss.
key_facts: PALISADE-4 was a public speaking challenge trial of intranasal fasedienol for acute treatment of social anxiety disorder; did not demonstrate statistically significant improvement on primary endpoint of anxiety reduction measured by SUDS vs placebo; mirrors the earlier PALISADE-3 failure; safety/tolerability consistent with prior studies; company plans to meet FDA about a registrational pathway using a single future Phase 3 with the Liebowitz Social Anxiety Scale as primary endpoint; stock closed at $0.22, down >70% on June 30; two consecutive Phase 3 misses

title: FDA Rejects Unicycive (Second Time) and Sobi on Manufacturing
headline: The FDA issued complete response letters to Unicycive and Sobi on manufacturing grounds — Unicycive's second rejection for the same third-party plant issue.
key_facts: Unicycive received its second CRL for its kidney disease/hyperphosphatemia drug due to third-party manufacturing plant shortfalls; stock dropped over 50%; CEO Shalabh Gupta cites active discussion with FDA on label and packaging and optimism about a successful third-party vendor inspection and expeditious NDA resubmission; Sobi and Lantheus also had approval bids turned away over manufacturing concerns (June 29); part of a broader pattern of FDA scrutiny on contract manufacturing organizations; an FDA citizen petition has called on the agency to tighten CRL disclosure practices

title: BioCryst Ends All Internal Discovery, Closes Alabama Facility
headline: BioCryst is shuttering all internal discovery programs and closing its Birmingham, Alabama Discovery Center of Excellence by end of 2026, pivoting to external innovation partnerships.
key_facts: Announced June 29, 2026; follows termination of avoralstat development for diabetic macular edema one month prior; company called it a 'strategic evolution' to focus on external innovation and partnerships for its rare disease pipeline; Birmingham facility to close by end of 2026; ticker BCRX; retains commercialized assets (Orladeyo/berotralstat for HAE) while outsourcing all early-stage R&D

title: Sanofi Nexviazyme Hits Phase 3 Endpoint in Infantile-Onset Pompe
headline: Sanofi's Nexviazyme (avalglucosidase alfa) met its primary endpoint in a Phase 3 trial in treatment-naive infantile-onset Pompe disease patients aged six months and under.
key_facts: Primary endpoint: proportion of patients alive and free of invasive ventilation at 52 weeks; population: treatment-naive pediatric participants six months of age and younger with infantile-onset Pompe disease (IOPD); announced June 30, 2026; expands Nexviazyme's data package in the most severe Pompe population

title: Viridian Wins FDA Approval for Lumvoa in Thyroid Eye Disease
headline: Viridian Therapeutics secured FDA approval for Lumvoa in thyroid eye disease, setting up competition with Amgen's blockbuster Tepezza.
key_facts: FDA approval for Lumvoa to treat thyroid eye disease (TED); triggers competition with Amgen's blockbuster Tepezza

title: FDA Cell and Gene Therapy Head Steps Down After One Year
headline: The FDA's cell and gene therapy head stepped down after one year in the role.
key_facts: FDA cell and gene therapy head steps down after 1 year in role; BioSpace, June 30

title: Gilead Lays Off 108 Employees
headline: Gilead laid off 108 employees effective June 30.
key_facts: 108 employees laid off effective June 30; BioSpace Layoff Tracker

title: Pharma M&A Tops $65B in Q1 2026 — Best Quarter Since 2020
headline: PwC reported more than $65 billion in pharma and life sciences M&A in Q1 2026, the best quarter since 2020.
key_facts: >$65 billion in pharma/life sciences M&A in Q1 2026; best quarter since 2020; PwC report via Yahoo Finance/Motley Fool, June 27; listed under Additional Notable Items as background

title: Can-Fite Jumps on Japan Patent Allowance for Namodenoson
headline: Can-Fite BioPharma stock jumped after the Japan Patent Office allowed a patent covering A3 adenosine receptor agonists, including Namodenoson, for obesity and metabolic disorders.
key_facts: Stock jumped June 26 after Japan Patent Office allowed patent covering A3 adenosine receptor agonists (including Namodenoson) for obesity/metabolic disorders; listed under Additional Notable Items as background

title: Bayer Stock Surges to New Heights
headline: Bayer stock surged to new heights on June 25, with momentum continuing into the week's end; reason not fully specified.
key_facts: Stock surged to new heights June 25; reason not fully specified but momentum continued into week-end; Benzinga; listed under Additional Notable Items as background
